_README: Panel member #1 (Claude) candidate fill for greeceforamericans, scoped to article-list.json. Working material — NEVER pushed. Statuses set honestly: agreed_stable for confident stable facts; verify_recommended for YMYL/change-prone (ETIAS, advisory, driving); serve_live for volatile (rates, flight prices, daily budgets). Reconciled later with external models into facts.json.

locale.money_framing: express costs in USD with EUR equivalents

_meta.reconciliation_note: 46 flagged fields canonicalized; 0 genuine factual conflicts among the 4 models. YMYL facts kept verify_recommended; volatile kept serve_live. 5 open questions remain for official verification.

_fact_field_shape: Every FACT uses: { value, source_url, status, notes }. source_url null where not officially checked (provisional per Stage-2 rules).

name_local.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

value: Greek
EET (UTC+2), EEST (UTC+3) in summer
timezones.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

power_plug_voltage.value: 230V, 50Hz, Type C and F (Americans need a plug adapter; 120V-only devices need a converter)
power_plug_voltage.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

visa._note: YMYL + change-prone. ETIAS is the big upcoming change for Americans — flag hard.

max_stay_days.value: 90 days within any 180-day period (Schengen-wide, not per country)

fee.value: No visa fee. ETIAS, once required, is expected to cost about €20 (free for under-18 and over-70).

processing_time.value: ETIAS expected to approve most applications within minutes to 96 hours

online_authorization_required.value: ETIAS travel authorization is expected to become mandatory for Americans during 2025–2026 — verify current status before travel. The EU Entry/Exit System (EES) biometric border checks are also rolling out.
online_authorization_required.notes: ETIAS is NOT a visa; it's a pre-travel authorization like the US ESTA. Date has slipped repeatedly — MUST verify live.

type.notes: US citizens do not need a visa for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period under Schengen rules.

fee.notes: No visa fee for visa-free entry.

online_authorization_required.notes: ETIAS is not yet required; expected to launch in the last quarter of 2026. Once active, US citizens will need to obtain ETIAS authorization before travel.
e.g. ETA / Visit Japan Web / AEVM even when visa-free

type.notes: Up to 90 days in 180 for tourism/business

value: Unofficial 'ETIAS' and 'Schengen visa' sites that charge inflated fees — only use the official EU europa.eu domain
scam_sites_to_avoid.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

passport_validity_required.value: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from the Schengen area, and issued within the previous 10 years
passport_validity_required.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

onward_ticket_required.value: Not officially required for US citizens, but airlines or border officers may ask for proof of onward/return travel.
onward_ticket_required.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

notes.value: Carry proof of accommodation and sufficient funds; border officers can ask.

approx_rate_note.value: As of mid-June 2026, approximately 1 EUR = 1.16 USD (USD 1 ≈ €0.86).
approx_rate_note.notes: VOLATILE — USD/EUR rate served live, never frozen into prose

cash_vs_card.value: Cards (incl. contactless, Apple/Google Pay) are widely accepted in cities, hotels, and most tavernas. Carry some cash for small islands, village kafenia, markets, and some ferries.
cash_vs_card.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

atm_availability.value: ATMs plentiful in Athens and on main islands. Prefer bank ATMs; AVOID Euronet ATMs (poor rates/fees). Always choose to be charged in EUR, not USD (decline dynamic currency conversion).
atm_availability.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

currency_import_export_limit.value: Amounts of €10,000 or more (cash equivalent) must be declared when entering/leaving the EU
currency_import_export_limit.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

tipping_norm.value: Tipping is modest and not obligatory. Rounding up or leaving 5–10% at tavernas is appreciated; a few euros for hotel staff and taxi rounding. No US-style 18–20% expectation.
tipping_norm.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

value: Athens Eleftherios Venizelos International Airport (ATH)
Heraklion Nikos Kazantzakis (HER), Crete
Thessaloniki (SKG)
seasonal island airports: Santorini (JTR), Mykonos (JMK)
main_international_airports.notes: ATH is the primary US gateway
main_international_airports.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

national_carrier.value: Aegean Airlines (with Olympic Air for domestic)
national_carrier.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

value: Nonstop seasonal/year-round from JFK, EWR, ORD, ATL, IAD, PHL, BOS to ATH on carriers incl. Emirates (EWR/JFK), Delta, American, United, Aegean; ~9–11 hours eastbound depending on origin
flight_from_audience_origins.notes: Durations stable; PRICES serve_live. Nonstop routes are mostly seasonal (roughly May–Oct).

intercity_rail.value: Limited rail network (Athens–Thessaloniki main line). Not the backbone for a first-timer island trip; ferries and domestic flights matter more.
intercity_rail.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

buses.value: KTEL intercity buses serve the mainland; on islands local buses are cheap but infrequent. KTEL for Athens-area day trips (e.g., Cape Sounion).
buses.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

domestic_flights.value: Aegean, Olympic Air, Sky Express fly Athens to Santorini, Mykonos, Crete, Naxos, Paros (~45–55 min). Often faster than ferry for far islands like Crete; book ahead in summer.
domestic_flights.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

ride_sharing.value: Uber operates in Athens but dispatches licensed taxis (not private drivers); the local 'FREENOW' app is widely used. On islands, taxis are scarce — pre-book transfers.
ride_sharing.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

car_rental_and_driving.value: Greeks drive on the RIGHT. US license accepted in practice by many agencies, but Greek law requires an International Driving Permit (IDP) alongside it — get one from AAA before you go. Minimum rental age usually 21–25. Roads on islands can be narrow/steep; a car is great on Crete and Naxos, unnecessary on Santorini/Mykonos.
car_rental_and_driving.notes: IDP requirement is the load-bearing American-specific fact; drive-on-right; BAC limit 0.05%.
car_rental_and_driving.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

climate_by_season.value: Mediterranean: hot dry summers (Jul–Aug highs 85–95°F/29–35°C, peak crowds), mild wet winters. Sea is warmest Aug–Sep. The meltemi wind can disrupt Cyclades ferries in Jul–Aug.
climate_by_season.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

best_months.notes: RECOMMENDATION — shoulder season: warm, swimmable, fewer crowds, lower prices. The site's sweet-spot recommendation for first-timers.

value.name: Mid-July to late August (heat, crowds, highest prices, meltemi wind)
Winter for islands (many hotels/restaurants on small islands close Nov–Mar; Santorini/Mykonos partly shut down)
August 15 (Assumption of Mary - major holiday, ferries and accommodations book up)
Late July and August (intense heat, peak crowds, highest prices)
Late November through March for islands (most hotels/ferries shut down)

avoid_periods.notes: RECOMMENDATION. Orthodox Easter (date varies yearly) brings domestic travel surges — verify the date each year.

safety_health._note: YMYL. Greece is generally very safe for tourists; advisory level change-prone.

advisory_level.value: Typically US State Department Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) — verify current level and date before travel
advisory_level.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

main_risk.value: Petty theft/pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas (Athens metro, Monastiraki, Acropolis crowds); occasional strikes/demonstrations in Athens; summer heat and wildfire risk inland.
main_risk.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

vaccines.value: No special vaccines required for US travelers; routine vaccinations recommended. Check CDC before travel.
vaccines.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

tap_water_safe.value: Tap water is safe to drink in Athens and most of the mainland. On many islands (incl. Santorini, Mykonos) tap water is brackish/desalinated — locals drink bottled; fine for brushing teeth.
tap_water_safe.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

emergency_numbers.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

value: Taxi overcharging from Athens airport — agree on meter or fixed tariff
Bar 'friendly local' scams in Athens nightlife districts
Euronet ATM fee/rate gouging
Inflated 'skip-the-line' resellers near the Acropolis
common_scams.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

sim_esim.value: eSIM is the easiest option for Americans — Airalo, Holafly, or local Cosmote/Vodafone/Nova eSIMs work well. EU roaming means a Greek SIM works across the EU. Verizon/AT&T/T-Mobile international day passes are pricier but simplest.
sim_esim.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

coverage.value: Excellent 4G/5G in cities and on main islands; can be patchy in remote mountain/coastal spots on Crete and smaller Cyclades.
coverage.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

costs._note: Approximate USD ranges per person/day excluding flights; serve_live and dated.

daily_budget_low.value: ~$70–110/day (hostels/budget rooms, ferries deck class, gyros & casual tavernas, public transit)

daily_budget_mid.value: ~$150–280/day (3–4★ hotels, mix of ferries/flights, sit-down tavernas, some tours)

daily_budget_high.value: ~$400+/day (4–5★ / cave suites, private transfers, fine dining, private tours)

dress_norms.value: Casual and beachy on islands; smart-casual for nicer dinners. Cover shoulders and knees to enter churches and monasteries (e.g., on Crete, in Athens). Bring sun protection and sturdy sandals for ancient-site stones.
dress_norms.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

value: Do say 'kaliméra' (good morning) / 'efcharistó' (thank you)
Do expect late dinners (9pm+) and a slow, lingering meal pace
Don't rush the waiter for the check — you ask for it
Don't flush toilet paper where bins are provided (older island plumbing)
Don't expect everything open midday — some shops close for afternoon rest
dos_and_donts.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

how_to_get_there.value: Primary US gateway (ATH). Metro Line 3 connects the airport to the city center (~40 min, ~€9); taxi ~€40 flat daytime tariff to the center.
how_to_get_there.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

local_transport.value: Walkable historic core (Plaka, Monastiraki). Clean, cheap metro reaches the Acropolis, port (Piraeus), and airport. Single ticket ~€1.20.
local_transport.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

airport.reconciliation: canonicalized from 4-model panel (all agreed on substance)

local_transport.value: A rental car is the best way to see Crete; KTEL buses link main towns along the north coast. Distances are large (Chania to Heraklion ~2h drive).

itineraries.title: The Perfect 10-Day Greece Itinerary for First-Timers

value.evening: Rooftop dinner with Acropolis view; day 3 Cape Sounion sunset

value.morning: Ferry to Santorini; settle in Oia/Imerovigli

value.evening: Ancient Agora and sunset from Areopagus Hill.

value.morning: Day trip to Delphi or Cape Sounion.
Boat tour to Nea Kameni and hot springs.

value.afternoon: Beach time at Paradise or Super Paradise.

value.morning: Arrive in Athens, settle into hotel.
Guided tour of the Acropolis and Parthenon.
value.afternoon: Acropolis Museum and lunch in Koukaki.
Check into hotel, explore Fira or Imerovigli.

value.morning: Hike from Fira to Oia along the caldera edge.
Catamaran sailing cruise in the caldera.
value.afternoon: Swim near the hot springs (on the cruise).
value.evening: Wine tasting at a local Santorini vineyard.

value.morning: High-speed ferry to Naxos or Paros.
value.afternoon: Explore the port town (Chora) and local shops.

value.morning: Rent a car to explore inland mountain villages.
value.afternoon: Relax at a sandy beach (Agios Prokopios in Naxos or Kolymbithres in Paros).

value.morning: Beach day or visit historical ruins (Portara in Naxos).
value.afternoon: Last minute shopping and island wandering.

value.morning: Flight or ferry back to Athens.

plan.notes: RECOMMENDATION — Athens + Naxos (relaxed, authentic) + Santorini (the wow finish). Naxos can be swapped for Paros.

assumptions.value: Shoulder-season travel; one-way ferries forward, fly back from Santorini to save backtracking.

value.morning: Fly or ferry back to Athens.

itineraries.title: 5 Days in Greece: A Short First Trip That Works

itineraries.title: Athens and Santorini in One Week

assumptions.value: Focuses solely on Athens and Santorini; a classic and romantic combo.

value.morning: Fly or ferry back to Athens.

assumptions.value: The classic route: Athens → Mykonos → Naxos → Paros → Santorini → Athens. Covers the most popular Cyclades islands.
